title: eriophorum lockhartii
intro: Eriophorum lockhartii, also known as Lockhart's cotton grass, is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The roots and rhizomes of Eriophorum lockhartii have been used to treat various health issues, including sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations, and are also believed to have astringent and diuretic properties. Eriophorum lockhartii can be prepared into herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin conditions.
